card.intuition: The technical decision-making of exactly HOW to split your code: by Route (page-level), by Component (lazy loading heavy parts), or by Vendor (separating libraries from your code).
card.analogy: Packing a car for a road trip: do you put everything in one huge box (One Bundle)? No, you put 'Driving Snacks' in the front seat (Route logic), 'Camping Gear' in the trunk (Lazy components), and 'Tools/Spare Tire' in the hidden compartment (Vendor libraries).
when_to_use: Large-scale apps with huge 3rd-party dependencies (like Google Maps or D3.js)
Optimizing 'Long Term Caching' (vendor files change less often than your code)
gotchas: Vendor Splitting is vital: if you change one line of your CSS, you don't want the user to have to re-download 200KB of React and Lodash
Watch out for 'Shared Dependencies': if Page A and Page B both use a heavy library, make sure it's in its own separate chunk so it's only downloaded once

exercise.prompt: Identify the common practice of splitting code based on the current URL path.
exercise.starter_code:
def identify_strategy():
    # Splitting so that /login and /dashboard load separate files
    strategy = "_____ Based Splitting"
    return strategy
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "route"
assert check("Route")
print("PASS")
